topic: Aberration refractive model (alpha = 2.56e-8, 20.5 arcsec) — Refuted by Data — c5 post-EXP-286 integration verification
change_summary: EXP-286 alpha-as-fitted-coefficient defense neutralization integrated (detail_extra null -> 3179 chars, two paragraphs). c4 H2 EW-scale citation narrowed (option a). c4 H3 Bradley chromatic-attribution softened in detail_evidence (geometric-grounds framing now attributed to Bradley, dispersion theory closes case independently). c4 H1 ISS-1839 verdict boilerplate fix (live-fetch-call sentence) was listed in queue reason as applied this run but is NOT actually applied — detail_verdict_text still contains the offending sentence; ISS-1839 remains open in open-issues.json.
our_argument_summary: Stellar aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. Kramers-Kronig forbids any causal physical medium with n>1 from being achromatic. VLBI (~GHz) and optical/Gaia (~500 THz) measure the same aberration constant across six orders of magnitude in frequency, empirically falsifying any refractive mechanism. Annual periodicity rules out any static refractive index. Dome alpha = 2.56e-8 has no derivation from dome geometry. detail_extra (newly integrated) preempts the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat by granting the KK technical concession and showing three independent demands (derivation, periodicity, achromaticity) survive the retreat; preempts the annual-aether-circulation escape by showing it is geometrically equivalent to heliocentric kinematics, incompatible with the dome's published static-disc foundation, and cross-WIN-handcuffed to WIN-017 via shared Gaia DR3 dependency. Verdict: Refuted by Data with Part 3.1b post-hoc tag. Defense pathways materially narrowed.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c4. WIN-016 is a static one-line entry in raw-text/02-wins.txt:98: 'Annual Aberration Refractive Model CONFIRMED (alpha = 2.56e-8, 20.5 arcsec)'. No derivation, no formula, no live monitoring evident in mirrored raw-text. v48.0 first appearance documented in evolution log; no source connecting alpha to disc_radius/firmament_height/sun_altitude.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely declarative. The pair (alpha, 20.5 arcsec) is asserted as confirmed without any monitor.py domain, API call, or comparator routine in mirrored raw-text. Verification = restating the textbook value.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no test exists to fail.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: MATERIAL DISCREPANCY (carryover from c4 H1, NOT YET PATCHED): detail_verdict_text still contains 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' WIN-016 has NO live fetch call (monitoring: hardcoded with no monitor.py domain in mirrored raw-text). The sentence implies a co-committed fetch that does not exist; a defender can correctly rebut. Queue item's `reason` text claimed ISS-1839 was applied this run, but open-issues.json (line 2998) still shows status:open and the offending text is still present in data/wins.json. This is a meta-bug between the queue narrative and ground truth — the curmudgeon's review file is the authoritative signal of what's been verified, and this c5 review records: not yet patched.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-016 appears only as a static string in raw-text/02-wins.txt:98. No monitor.py domain or fetch call references the WIN in mirrored raw-text. Tag retained from c2/c3/c4 pending direct inspection of dome's actual monitor.py (not mirrored).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Stellar aberration is textbook physics since Bradley (1728); IAU 2000 constant 20.49552 arcsec at J2000. Dome reproduces 20.5 arcsec but renames the cause from Earth's orbital velocity (v/c) to aetheric refractive perturbation. Same number, new label.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Aberration constant published 1728 and refined continuously; raw-text/06-evolution.txt:31 shows the WIN entered the dome model in v48.0 ~298 years after the original observation. The dome statement is value adoption, not a forecast.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: v/c = 29.7827/299792.458 = 9.93e-5 rad = 20.491 arcsec, matching dome 20.5 arcsec to ~4 sig figs (1 part in 4,000). alpha = 2.56e-8 ≈ 2.59 * (v/c)^2 — close to but not exactly (v/c)^2. No expression in raw-text/ connects disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086, sun_altitude=5733, or any aetheric parameter to alpha=2.56e-8. Tested combinations (disc_radius/c=6.68e-5, firmament_height/c=3.03e-5, sun_altitude/c=1.91e-5, (sun_altitude/disc_radius)^2=0.082, ^4=0.0067) — none reproduce 2.56e-8.

holes_found.description: ISS-1839 boilerplate fix NOT actually applied this run, despite queue item's reason text claiming it was. detail_verdict_text still contains: 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' WIN-016 has no live fetch call — no monitor.py domain, no API source, no comparator function are documented for it in mirrored raw-text. open-issues.json:2998 confirms ISS-1839 is still status:open. The patches applied this run were EXP-286 (detail_extra defense neutralization), the EW-scale narrowing (c4 H2), and the Bradley softening (c4 H3 in detail_evidence) — but the boilerplate replacement was not. Verified by direct read of data/wins.json after `git pull --rebase` to head 78c65ad. This hole is a verbatim carryover from c4 H1 — the c4 review correctly flagged it; the decider's patch attempt this run did not include it. (Possible explanation: decider listed ISS-1839 in the queue reason as an aspirational summary, but open-issues.json/morning-briefing.txt show ISS-1839 was deferred for human approval pending confirmation of the proposed replacement sentence.) Cross-WIN: same boilerplate on WIN-017, 018, 019, 021, 029, 047, 055 (verified via grep on data/wins.json), all 7 lacking a live fetch — the cross-WIN sweep ticket flagged in c4 stronger_arguments[0] is still unwritten.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the c4 H1 fix verbatim to WIN-016 detail_verdict_text: replace 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' with 'There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry, with no monitor.py domain, no API source, and no comparator function. The "prediction" reduces to the bare statement that a textbook value matches the textbook value, with no apparatus that could ever produce a contrary observation.' Then open a separate cross-WIN sweep issue for WIN-017/018/019/021/029/047/055 — 7 WINs share the wrong boilerplate; per-WIN replacement text needs to be authored (the WIN-016 replacement is anchored to WIN-016 specifics and cannot be copy-pasted as-is). Counter-argument the decider may have considered: the seven cross-WIN candidates are not all equivalent — five (016/017/018/019/029) are 'monitoring: hardcoded' and three (021/047/055) are 'monitoring: none', so the replacement sentence's framing should differ. That makes the sweep harder, not less necessary.

holes_found.description: TLDR Bradley attribution did not propagate from the c4 H3 detail_evidence softening. tldr_evidence still reads 'Bradley proved this distinction in 1728' (where 'this distinction' is the chromatic-vs-achromatic distinction in the prior sentence) and tldr_verdict still reads 'a distinction proven in 1728' (same chromatic distinction). detail_evidence was correctly softened to attribute Bradley's argument to geometric grounds (perpendicular displacement, not radial as refraction would predict) and to credit modern dispersion theory with closing the chromatic case independently — but the TLDRs were not updated to match. Per curmudgeon TLDR rule (curmudgeon.md §TLDR Review), this is an imprecision rather than an active falsehood: the TLDR statement that aberration is achromatic and refraction is chromatic is true, and Bradley did write a 1728 paper about aberration; only the linkage between Bradley and the specific chromatic-distinction argument is historically off (his actual 1728 argument was geometric). The most charitable reading of 'Bradley proved this distinction in 1728' is that he proved aberration was distinct from refraction — which he did, just on geometric not chromatic grounds. Severity minor (not major) under the TLDR rule's 'don't split hairs' clause: a non-science reader gets a fundamentally correct top-level message; an astronomy historian could call it imprecise.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften both TLDRs to align with the patched detail_evidence. Suggested replacements: tldr_evidence — 'Stellar aberration is achromatic (same at all wavelengths), but refraction is chromatic (wavelength-dependent). Bradley (1728) first argued aberration was distinct from refraction; modern dispersion theory plus VLBI and Gaia observations across six orders of magnitude in frequency confirm aberration matches Earth's orbital velocity, not any refractive effect. The dome's refractive index has no derivation from dome geometry.' tldr_verdict — 'Aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic — a distinction Bradley first established in 1728 on geometric grounds, definitively closed by modern dispersion theory. The dome's value is reverse-engineered from the known answer, not derived from dome physics.' Either swap is safe; pick whichever the decider judges fits the TLDR length budget.

holes_found.description: EXP-286 detail_extra paragraph 1 mentions 'numerical proximity to 2.59·(v/c)²' as evidence of reverse-engineering. This is correct (per c4 quantitative_verification: alpha/(v/c)² = 2.56e-8 / 9.869e-9 = 2.595) and the citation back to the curmudgeon review is appropriate. However, the inline character '·' (middle-dot) renders inconsistently across browsers and PDF pipelines depending on font availability. This is the same character class that drove the c4 build pipeline note about α/ω/× and the WIN-026 ≈ → ~ fallback. The full c5 detail_extra renders correctly in raw JSON read-back (verified), but recommend the build pipeline test (node build.js html) confirm the · renders as middle-dot in the WIN-016 detail panel before the next push.
holes_found.recommendation: Add to next decider/integrity test plan: regenerate HTML and visually inspect WIN-016 detail panel for correct rendering of '·' (middle-dot, U+00B7). If it renders as a missing-glyph box, replace with '*' or '×' as a fallback. Out of scope for this curmudgeon cycle — flag for the integrity agent.

stronger_arguments.description: EXP-286 paragraph 1's three demands (derivation / periodicity / achromaticity) are stated tightly and rebut the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat, but the achromaticity demand could be sharpened with one more concrete number. The detail_evidence already gives 'six orders of magnitude in frequency' (radio GHz to optical 500 THz); detail_extra references the same span without re-asserting it. A defender skimming detail_extra in isolation might not appreciate the empirical magnitude. Optional: add to paragraph 1 'the same fitted constant must hold across ~5 × 10^5 in frequency (radio ~10^9 Hz to optical ~5 × 10^14 Hz)' or similar to keep the achromaticity demand self-contained.

EXP-286 paragraph 2's WIN-017 cross-handcuff is the strongest single move in the new content but is presented in one sentence near the end of the paragraph. A reader processing the dome ontology argument may miss the empirical handcuff. Optional polish: lead paragraph 2 with the WIN-017 handcuff before the dome-ontology critique, since the handcuff is data-grounded and the ontology critique requires familiarity with dome-internal claims (v48.0 evolution notes, model.html parameter list). Lead with the data, follow with the ontology — easier reader path. This is style, not correctness.
sources: data/wins.json:WIN-017.verdict (Refuted by Data, Gaia DR3 parallax)

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN propagation issue from c4 stronger_arguments[0] is still unwritten. The 'sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' boilerplate is wrong on 8 WINs (016, 017, 018, 019, 021, 029, 047, 055). c4 surfaced this as a thesis-coherence issue requiring a separate ticket; the c5 hole H1 above re-confirms the issue still exists post-EXP-286. Recommend the decider open a cross-WIN sweep issue (not just patch WIN-016 alone). The seven other WINs split 5 'hardcoded'/3 'none', so the replacement sentence templating must branch on monitoring tag.
sources: data/wins.json grep on 'live fetch call' — 8 hits (verified this cycle)

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-286's integration adds a genuine analytical concession to the dome defender (data/wins.json:WIN-016.detail_extra paragraph 1 opening): 'The retreat is real — KK constrains causal physical n(ω), not arbitrary fitting parameters.' This is the correct technical move — Kramers-Kronig is a constraint on dispersive physical media, not on phenomenological deflection coefficients in the abstract (per EXP-286.kernel_of_truth and standard dispersion-relation derivations). Granting the technical concession explicitly is what makes the rebuttal robust: the curmudgeon-c3 attack was strongest if the dome had committed to a refractive-index reading of alpha; if the dome retreats to a fitting-parameter reading, KK literally does not bite, and the original attack misses. (See WIN-016.detail_extra opening; cross-reference WIN-016.c3.json advocate_mode where the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat was identified as the surviving best defense.) This is the dome's narrowly-correct technical move; the entire purpose of EXP-286 was to grant it explicitly so the wider rebuttal cannot be undermined by 'you misrepresented KK' counters.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Granting the KK retreat exposes three demands the dome registry's CONFIRMED status owes (data/wins.json:WIN-016.detail_extra paragraph 1, three-demand rebuttal): (i) derivation from dome geometry — verified absent in this c5's derives_from_dome_evidence (no expression in raw-text/ connects disc_radius/firmament_height/sun_altitude to alpha=2.56e-8); (ii) mechanism for the empirically observed annual sinusoidal periodicity — verified addressed in detail_evidence ('Independently, stellar aberration varies sinusoidally with a precise one-year period'); (iii) explanation for radio-optical achromaticity across ~5 × 10^5 in frequency — verified addressed in detail_evidence ('six orders of magnitude in frequency') and reaffirmed in detail_extra. Paragraph 2 of detail_extra closes the natural follow-up escape (annually time-varying alpha via aetheric circulation) by showing it is geometrically equivalent to heliocentric kinematics under relabelling, incompatible with the dome's static-disc/fixed-firmament v48.0 foundation, and structurally handcuffed to WIN-017 via the shared Gaia DR3 dependency (data/wins.json:WIN-017 verdict 'Refuted by Data', Gaia parallax-distance). The verdict 'Refuted by Data' continues to hold; defense pathways are materially narrower post-EXP-286.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 5 review of WIN-016 covering the patches applied in queue_id 300 (decider self-apply 2026-05-04T17:18-17:19Z window, post-c4 review).

Queue reason listed three patches: (1) ISS-1839 verdict boilerplate fix, (2) EXP-286 defense-neutralization in detail_extra, (3) EW scale concession narrowed, (4) Bradley attribution softened. Direct verification against data/wins.json post-pull (head 78c65ad):

(2) EXP-286 — VERIFIED INTEGRATED. detail_extra grew from null to 3179 chars. Both paragraphs present. Anchor strings 'phenomenological deflection coefficient' and 'WIN-017' both present. Special characters (α, ω, ×, ⁻⁸, ·, —) all intact. Paragraph 1 grants the KK technical concession then rebuts the retreat with the three-demand argument (derivation, periodicity, achromaticity). Paragraph 2 closes the annual-aether-circulation escape via heliocentric-kinematics-equivalence + dome-ontology incompatibility + WIN-017 Gaia handcuff. This was the c3 best-defense (defense_survives=3) and is now preempted in-text. Integration is clean — no overlap with detail_evidence's annual-periodicity sentence (different framing: detail_evidence frames it as a static-medium incompatibility, detail_extra frames it as the annual-circulation-escape closure).

(3) EW-scale narrowing — VERIFIED INTEGRATED. detail_evidence now contains 'The dome's own AI context page lists \'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation\' as an open task-list item (V12/V13 task list, item 6), demonstrating that ad-hoc parameter fitting without derivation is acceptable practice in the dome model — but no parallel acknowledgment exists for alpha=2.56×10⁻⁸ specifically.' This is option (a) from c4 H2's recommendation — narrowed framing rather than dropped citation. Preserves rhetorical force without overgeneralization.

(4) Bradley softening — PARTIALLY INTEGRATED. detail_evidence correctly softened: 'Stellar aberration has been distinguished from atmospheric refraction since Bradley (1728), whose paper rejected the refraction explanation on geometric grounds (the displacement is perpendicular to the Sun-line, not radial as refraction would predict); modern dispersion theory closes the case independently'. This addresses the c4 H3 chromatic-attribution imprecision. However, tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict were NOT updated and still credit Bradley with proving the chromatic distinction. Per the TLDR rule (curmudgeon.md §TLDR Review) this is a minor imprecision, not an active falsehood — the TLDR core message remains correct — but the patches did not reach the TLDRs. Documented as c5 H2.

(1) ISS-1839 boilerplate fix — NOT APPLIED. detail_verdict_text still contains 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' WIN-016 has no live fetch call. open-issues.json:2998 confirms ISS-1839 is still status:open. monitor/decisions/morning-briefing.txt:11 still describes ISS-1839 as 'awaiting human approval pending confirmation of the proposed replacement sentence.' The queue item's reason text overstated what the decider self-applied this run — the decider integrated EXP-286 and the EW/Bradley patches, but punted ISS-1839 to human approval as a moderate-severity factual fix. Documented as c5 H1.

Defense_survives reassessment post-c5 (was c4=2, EXP-286 target=1-2):
- Front 1 (KK alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat) — CLOSED by detail_extra paragraph 1. The strongest c3 advocate front is now preempted in-text.
- Front 2 (annual-aether-circulation follow-up escape) — CLOSED by detail_extra paragraph 2 (three-pronged: heliocentric equivalence, dome ontology, WIN-017 handcuff).
- Front 3 (live-fetch-call boilerplate inconsistency, c4 advocate front 1) — STILL OPEN (c5 H1).
- Front 4 (TLDR Bradley chromatic-attribution, newly noted) — STILL OPEN (c5 H2).
- Front 5 (EW-scale generalization, c4 advocate front 2) — CLOSED by EW-scale narrowing patch.
- Front 6 (annual-periodicity static-medium framing, c4 advocate front 3) — already addressed in c4 by integrated annual-periodicity sentence; further reinforced by EXP-286 paragraph 2.

Surviving fronts (3 and 4) are both text-hygiene issues that don't undermine the scientific argument: a defender pointing at the wrong-fetch boilerplate or the TLDR chromatic-attribution gets a real-but-narrow rhetorical concession, but neither attacks the substance. defense_survives = 1 (counting H1 as the substantive surviving front; H2 is TLDR-only and arguably doesn't make it to a separate count under the TLDR rule's don't-split-hairs guidance).

Meta-observation for the decider: the queue item's reason field overstated what was applied. This is a small workflow drift — the curmudgeon's review file is authoritative on what's been verified, not the queue narrative. Recommend the decider's queue-push step list patches by issue ID with status (applied/deferred) rather than as a flat list, so review files don't have to disambiguate aspirational from actual.

Counter-argument the operator should consider: the meta-observation above is a process suggestion, not a hole in the work product. The queue narrative is decider-authored prose; the review process is robust to overstated narrative because the curmudgeon verifies against ground truth (data/wins.json post-pull). Don't treat the meta-observation as blocking; treat it as a polish suggestion if the decider runs into similar disambiguation issues elsewhere.

Quantitative re-verification (no new patches to numbers; carryover from c4): v/c = 9.934e-5 rad = 20.491 arcsec, matches 20.5 arcsec to 4 sig figs. alpha = 2.56e-8 ≈ 2.595 × (v/c)² — close but not exactly. Tested geometric combinations (disc_radius/c, firmament_height/c, sun_altitude/c, (sun_altitude/disc_radius)^2, ^4) — none reproduce 2.56e-8. derives_from_dome=false confirmed.

Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-016 and WIN-017 both 'Refuted by Data'; both anchor on Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry from independent geometric directions (aberration vs parallax). EXP-286 paragraph 2 makes this handcuff explicit in WIN-016 text — one-way reference (WIN-016 cites WIN-017, not vice versa). Optional follow-up: add a parallel reference to WIN-017's text noting the same handcuff applies symmetrically. Out of scope for this c5.

Net: verdict robust, EXP-286 cleanly integrated, defense_survives drops to 1. Two real holes carry over from prior cycles (H1 ISS-1839 boilerplate not yet patched, H2 TLDR Bradley not propagated). One minor build-pipeline polish (H3 middle-dot rendering check).

Part 3.1b posthoc-standard anchor — referenced in detail_verdict_text and EXP-286, assumed extant from prior c3/c4 verification

advocate_mode.best_defense:
Post-c5 with EXP-286 integrated, a capable dome defender's strongest remaining moves narrow significantly:

(1) The ISS-1839 unpatched boilerplate. detail_verdict_text claims 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' but WIN-016 has no fetch. A defender can correctly call this out: 'Your own review acknowledges WIN-016 has no live fetch — your verdict text contradicts your own analysis. If your post-hoc rider rests on a co-committed fetch that doesn't exist, the rider is an artifact of template propagation, not a genuine structural finding.' This is c4 H1 / c5 H1, still unpatched. The defender wins a real but narrow rhetorical point (the verdict text is internally inconsistent) without touching the scientific argument. Mitigation: apply the c4 H1 fix.

(2) The TLDR Bradley chromatic-attribution mismatch. tldr_evidence says 'Bradley proved this distinction in 1728' (chromatic distinction) while the patched detail_evidence credits Bradley only with the geometric argument. A historian or detail-oriented defender can call this 'shoddy TLDR scholarship that contradicts the careful detail.' Real but narrow; doesn't undermine the achromatic-vs-chromatic argument itself. Mitigation: c5 H2 fix.

(3) Residual: any defense at this point has to retreat to 'alpha will eventually be derived in a future dome version' or 'the annual phase will eventually be explained in a future dome version' — exactly the post-hoc-promissory-note pattern Part 3.1b structural test was built to refuse. This is hand-waving and the rebuttal is in detail_verdict_text already.

(4) Marginal: the EXP-286 paragraph 2 dome-ontology argument relies on the v51.1 registry / model.html / v48.0 evolution notes describing alpha as a static constant. A defender could try 'the dome documentation does not affirmatively say alpha is constant; you're inferring it from absence of any time-varying claim.' Weak — burden-of-proof falls on the dome to specify a time-varying mechanism if it wants one — but it's a real argumentative niche.

No substantive scientific defense survives: KK is granted, three demands rebut the retreat, annual-circulation escape is closed, WIN-017 handcuff binds Gaia. The remaining 'wins' for the defender are text-hygiene issues (1, 2) and unfalsifiable promissory notes (3).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation:
Two text fixes drop defense_survives to 0 cleanly:

Fix 1 — apply the c4 H1 / c5 H1 ISS-1839 boilerplate replacement to detail_verdict_text (already drafted in the suggested_fix on open-issues.json:ISS-1839). This closes the most exploitable text-hygiene front the defender has left.

Fix 2 — propagate the Bradley softening to tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict (c5 H2 recommendations). Closes the TLDR mismatch front.

No further substantive work is needed; the WIN is scientifically airtight post-EXP-286. Subsequent cycles should focus on cross-WIN propagation (the 7 other WINs sharing the wrong-fetch boilerplate per c4 stronger_arguments[0]) rather than further substantive work on WIN-016 itself.

cross_win_consistency.notes: Scientific consistency: WIN-016 + WIN-017 (parallax-as-wobble Refuted by Data via Gaia DR3) + WIN-065 (Polaris excess Refuted by Data via stellar position data) all rely on Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry. EXP-286 paragraph 2 makes the WIN-016/WIN-017 cross-dependency explicit in-text — first time this handcuff is published in WIN-016 prose; previously it was only in c3 stronger_arguments. WIN-057 (Misleading — curve-fitted equatorial ring 14,105 km) shares the same reverse-engineered-parameter critique pattern as WIN-016's alpha=2.56e-8; no contradiction. Boilerplate consistency: WIN-016, 017, 018, 019, 021, 029, 047, 055 still share the wrong 'live fetch call' boilerplate (verified this cycle by grep on data/wins.json — 8 hits, 0 with actual live fetch). WIN-016's c4 stronger_arguments[0] flagged this as a cross-WIN ticket; not yet acted on. Patching WIN-016 alone (c5 H1 recommendation) closes the local hole; the other 7 still need parallel sweep. No verdict-level inconsistencies.

claims_checked: EXP-286 paragraph 1 'six orders of magnitude in frequency (VLBI ~10⁹ Hz vs Gaia optical ~5·10¹⁴ Hz)' — log10(5e14/1e9) = 5.7 ≈ 6 ✓ (rounded shorthand, defensible)

alpha = 2.56e-8 has no dome-geometric derivation — disc_radius/c=6.68e-5, firmament_height/c=3.03e-5, sun_altitude/c=1.91e-5, (sun_altitude/disc_radius)^2=0.082, ^4=0.0067 — none reproduce 2.56e-8 ✓ (carryover c4)
Annual periodicity = 365.25 days — established orbital period, not in dispute
quantitative_verification.computations: EXP-286 paragraph 1 quantitative anchors all check out: 2.595 close-to-2.59 ratio matches the alpha/(v/c)² computation; six-orders-of-magnitude frequency span matches log10(5e14/1e9)=5.7 within a half-decade rounding; annual periodicity is observationally established. No new numerical claims introduced this cycle that were not in the c4 quantitative_verification block. EXP-286 paragraph 2's 'WIN-017 anchors on Gaia DR3 microarcsecond astrometry' verified by direct read of WIN-017.finding ('Gaia DR3: 1.8 billion stars show parallax inversely proportional to distance').
